questions.prompt: A perpetuity pays $500 per year starting next year. The discount rate is 4%. What is its present value? Answer in dollars to the nearest cent.
A stock will pay a $3 dividend next year, growing 5% per year forever. The cost of equity is 11%. Using the Gordon growth model, what is the stock worth? Answer in dollars to the nearest cent.
A 30-year mortgage of $400,000 at a 7% annual rate, monthly compounding (rate per month = 0.07/12). What is the monthly payment? Answer in dollars to the nearest cent.
Which assumption does the Gordon growth model require?
questions.explanation: If g >= r, the discounted series diverges and PV is infinite (or undefined). The formula PV = C / (r-g) requires g < r.

furtherReading.title: Growth Perpetuities and the Dividend Discount Model
furtherReading.why: Open-textbook chapter that connects perpetuity formulas to Gordon growth and to mortgages/loans, hitting all three quiz themes together.
